title: MySQL container can't connect to Django container using podman compose
body:
### Issue Description

When I try to use Podman compose on two containers, a MySQL and a Django web app, I cannot get the MySQL container to start. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Using the django-mysql-docker Github repository, get rid of the spaces in the passwords in the .env.dev and set it up to be able to run
2. podman compose up


### Describe the results you received

podman-compose version: 1.0.6
['podman', '--version', '']
using podman version: 4.9.3
** excluding:  set()
['podman', 'inspect', '-t', 'image', '-f', '{{.Id}}', 'django-mysql-docker_web']
['podman', 'ps', '--filter', 'label=io.podman.compose.project=django-mysql-docker', '-a', '--format', '{{ index .Labels "io.podman.compose.config-hash"}}']
['podman', 'network', 'exists', 'django-mysql-docker_default']
podman create --name=mysql-container --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=e480ecdc9d2110dbef046189e9d4e7e7189cadfa505779bdbe550ce16fecfa4e --label io.podman.compose.project=django-mysql-docker --label io.podman.compose.version=1.0.6 --label PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=podman-compose@django-mysql-docker.service --label com.docker.compose.project=django-mysql-docker --label com.docker.compose.project.working_dir=/Users/ch807460/code/django-mysql-docker --label com.docker.compose.project.config_files=docker-compose.yml --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=db --env-file /Users/ch807460/code/django-mysql-docker/.env.dev -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD= -e MYSQL_USER= -e MYSQL_PASSWORD= -e MYSQL_DATABASE= -e MYSQL_ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD=0 -v /Users/ch807460/code/django-mysql-docker/mysql:/var/lib/mysql --net django-mysql-docker_default --network-alias db -p 3306:3306 --userns keep-id --restart always mysql:8.0 --default-authentication-plugin=CACHING_SHA2_PASSWORD --sql_mode=
9cdb7eca14c75d107e35c45190b63b839bd8d0989b131095b3fd1348cfb568c9
exit code: 0
['podman', 'network', 'exists', 'django-mysql-docker_default']
podman create --name=django-web --requires=mysql-container --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=e480ecdc9d2110dbef046189e9d4e7e7189cadfa505779bdbe550ce16fecfa4e --label io.podman.compose.project=django-mysql-docker --label io.podman.compose.version=1.0.6 --label PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=podman-compose@django-mysql-docker.service --label com.docker.compose.project=django-mysql-docker --label com.docker.compose.project.working_dir=/Users/ch807460/code/django-mysql-docker --label com.docker.compose.project.config_files=docker-compose.yml --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=web --env-file /Users/ch807460/code/django-mysql-docker/.env.dev -v /Users/ch807460/code/django-mysql-docker/app:/usr/src/app/ --net django-mysql-docker_default --network-alias web -p 8000:8000 django-mysql-docker_web python manage.py runserver 0.0.0.0:8000
9be79078a84aca286f357c1d63517dc03f83eb3268fa1f1078a6e8c3cc8bbd1e
exit code: 0
podman start -a mysql-container
Error: unable to start container 9cdb7eca14c75d107e35c45190b63b839bd8d0989b131095b3fd1348cfb568c9: preparing container 9cdb7eca14c75d107e35c45190b63b839bd8d0989b131095b3fd1348cfb568c9 for attach: something went wrong with the request: "listen tcp :3306: bind: address already in use\n"
exit code: 125


### Describe the results you expected

Expected to be able to open my browser to localhost:8000 and see the Django app

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

MacOS

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
> listen tcp :3306: bind: address already in use

The port is already used so you cannot use it.
When I run "lsof -i" it says nothing is running though @Luap99
